svg.title: Math foundations for ML — Stats, Linear Algebra, Calculus
svg.desc: A structured 6-week math refresh plan covering statistics, linear algebra, and calculus as they apply to machine learning and AI.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain mean, median, variance, standard deviation and probability distributions with ML examples. Keep it practical.')
sendPrompt('Explain vectors and matrices for ML beginners. Why do they matter? Include numpy code examples.')
sendPrompt('Explain derivatives and the concept of slope intuitively, as it applies to ML. Show simple Python examples.')
sendPrompt('Explain probability distributions — normal, bernoulli, binomial — with real ML use cases and visualizations in Python.')
sendPrompt('Explain matrix multiplication intuitively — why does it work that way? Show how it applies to neural network layers.')
sendPrompt('Explain partial derivatives and the chain rule intuitively. How does backpropagation use the chain rule?')
sendPrompt('Explain Bayes theorem intuitively with a practical ML example. How is it used in naive Bayes and probabilistic models?')
sendPrompt('Explain matrix transpose and inverse intuitively. When and why are they used in ML?')
sendPrompt('Explain gradient descent intuitively with a visual analogy. Show how partial derivatives are used to update weights.')
sendPrompt('Explain correlation, covariance and hypothesis testing as they apply to feature selection and model evaluation in ML.')
sendPrompt('Explain eigenvalues and eigenvectors intuitively. How are they used in PCA and dimensionality reduction?')
sendPrompt('Explain the gradient vector and how it generalizes derivatives to multiple dimensions. How does it guide weight updates in neural networks?')
sendPrompt('Explain MLE and MAP estimation intuitively. How do they relate to training loss functions like cross-entropy and MSE?')
sendPrompt('Explain vector spaces, norms (L1, L2), and cosine similarity. How are they used in embeddings and LLMs?')
sendPrompt('Explain the Jacobian and Hessian intuitively. When do they appear in deep learning?')
sendPrompt('Show me how linear regression uses all three math areas together — stats for loss, linear algebra for computation, calculus for optimization. Include Python code.')
sendPrompt('Show me how logistic regression and cross-entropy loss connect probability theory, matrix ops and gradient descent. Include Python code.')
sendPrompt('Show me how a simple neural network uses matrix multiplication for forward pass and chain rule for backprop. Build it from scratch in Python/NumPy.')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about 3Blue1Brown Essence of Linear Algebra and Essence of Calculus series. What topics do they cover?')
sendPrompt('Tell me about StatQuest with Josh Starmer. Which videos should I watch first for ML statistics?')
g.text: Best stats for ML — clear, no fluff

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me about the Mathematics for Machine Learning book. Is it good for someone refreshing, not learning from scratch?')
sendPrompt('Tell me about Khan Academy for math refresher. Which sections are most relevant for ML?')
sendPrompt('Give me a set of Python + NumPy exercises to practice linear algebra and calculus concepts for ML, one per week for 6 weeks.')
g.text: Practice rule: code every concept in NumPy the same day you learn it
Click for a 6-week Python + NumPy exercise set

g.onclick: sendPrompt('After completing the 6-week math refresh, what should I do first — start Scikit-learn or go straight into PyTorch?')
g.text: 6 weeks total · 45–60 min/day · weekends for coding practice
After this → Phase 1 ML (Scikit-learn + Spark MLlib on Databricks)
